description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story brick apartment building with a red-tiled gable roof and two prominent chimneys. The front facade features a symmetrical arrangement of windows with stone sills and lintels, and a central single-door entrance with a small stoop. The lower portion of the exterior walls is clad in a lighter stone or concrete material, providing a visual base. The rear of the building includes a modern, boxy extension with large windows, contrasting with the traditional brickwork. The side walls are plain, with no windows or doors, emphasizing the building's row-house or townhouse character. The overall appearance suggests a blend of traditional European residential architecture with some contemporary modifications.
description: A two-story brick apartment building with a red-tiled roof, symmetrical windows, and a central entrance, featuring a modern rear extension.
description_view_0: The front view shows a symmetrical brick facade with six windows on each floor, a central single-door entrance, and two chimneys on the roof.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain brick gable wall with no windows or doors, and the sloped red-tiled roof.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a modern extension with large windows, additional windows on the main building, and the continuation of the red-tiled roof.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain brick gable wall and the roof's slope, with part of the rear extension visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in two horizontal rows on the front facade, evenly spaced and aligned vertically, with additional windows on the rear and extension.
